title: Live Vercel API token committed in version-tracked .env.local files
impact: Credential exposure to any repo cloner, CI runner, or container image; allows Vercel project manipulation
evidence: HELIX_VERCEL_TOKEN=vcp_1Nb4zesMHVDtft3qtcWtGXUeTs7sE2nwzqYKiYcmbYg2FQ37a52szwxD present in apps/admin/.env.local, apps/docs/.env.local, and apps/storybook/.env.local
fix_suggestion: Rotate token immediately at vercel.com/account/tokens; remove from all .env.local files; store only in Vercel dashboard environment variables; create committed .env.local.example with placeholder values

title: hx-form @fires JSDoc documents wc-submit/wc-invalid/wc-reset but runtime dispatches hx-* events
impact: CEM manifest documents three event names that do not exist; Drupal and Storybook consumers listening for wc-submit/wc-invalid/wc-reset receive no events
evidence: JSDoc lines 24-26: @fires wc-submit, @fires wc-invalid, @fires wc-reset; runtime dispatchEvent at lines 218,238,252 uses hx-invalid, hx-submit, hx-reset
fix_suggestion: Update all three @fires tags to hx-submit, hx-invalid, hx-reset to match actual dispatched event names

title: hx-card @fires JSDoc documents wc-card-click but runtime dispatches hx-card-click
impact: CEM manifest documents wc-card-click which does not exist; consumers relying on CEM autodocs wire incorrect event listeners
evidence: Line 20 JSDoc: @fires {CustomEvent<...>} wc-card-click; line 91 runtime: new CustomEvent('hx-card-click', ...)
fix_suggestion: Change @fires annotation on line 20 to hx-card-click

title: hx-alert @fires JSDoc documents wc-after-close but runtime dispatches hx-after-close
impact: CEM manifest documents wc-after-close which never fires; consumers cannot detect alert dismissal via documented event name
evidence: Line 23 JSDoc: @fires {CustomEvent} wc-after-close; line 163 runtime: dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('hx-after-close', ...))
fix_suggestion: Update line 23 @fires tag to hx-after-close

title: Path traversal vulnerability in review date API endpoint via unsanitized date parameter
impact: Unauthenticated path traversal allows reading arbitrary .json files outside the reviews directory (e.g., GET /api/reviews/../../../../../../etc/passwd resolves to /etc/passwd.json)
evidence: loadReview(date) in issues-loader.ts:92 constructs path as join(REVIEWS_DIR, `${date}.json`) with no regex validation of date format; path.join() does not prevent directory escape
fix_suggestion: Validate date matches /^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/ before use; use path.resolve() and assert result starts with REVIEWS_DIR; add authentication check to the route

title: Admin app tsconfig.json does not extend tsconfig.base.json and is missing strict type flags
impact: Entire Next.js admin app has lower type-safety than the component library; noUncheckedIndexedAccess and exactOptionalPropertyTypes are not enforced, masking unsafe index access patterns
evidence: tsconfig.base.json sets noUncheckedIndexedAccess:true and exactOptionalPropertyTypes:true; apps/admin/tsconfig.json has no extends field and is missing both flags
fix_suggestion: Add noUncheckedIndexedAccess:true and exactOptionalPropertyTypes:true to apps/admin/tsconfig.json and fix resulting type errors

title: hx-card keyboard handler uses as unknown as MouseEvent double-cast producing structurally incorrect event detail
impact: KeyboardEvent cast as MouseEvent passes undefined for clientX/clientY/button properties; consumers of hx-card-click detail.originalEvent receive incorrect event object on keyboard activation
evidence: Line 104: this._handleClick(e as unknown as MouseEvent) where e is KeyboardEvent; _handleClick types its param as MouseEvent
fix_suggestion: Extract dispatchCardClick() method with no MouseEvent dependency; call from both _handleClick and _handleKeyDown

title: hx-radio sets role=radio on host but has no Space or Enter keyboard handler
impact: Radio buttons are completely inoperable via keyboard; pressing Space or Enter does nothing; violates WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.1 Keyboard (Level A)
evidence: connectedCallback lines 63-66 set role=radio on host; inner div has @click handler but no @keydown; tabIndex management done externally by hx-radio-group
fix_suggestion: Add keydown listener on host or inner div that calls _handleClick() when key === ' '; also handle Enter for consistency with WAI-ARIA radio pattern

title: IssueDetailPanel overlay missing role=dialog, aria-modal, and focus trap
impact: Keyboard users can Tab into background content while overlay is open; screen readers have no modal context; violates WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.1 (A) and SC 4.1.2 (A)
evidence: Panel renders as fixed inset-0 overlay with no role=dialog, no aria-modal=true, no aria-labelledby, no focus trap; backdrop onClick at line 131 has no keyboard handler
fix_suggestion: Add role=dialog, aria-modal=true, aria-labelledby to panel div; on open move focus inside panel; implement focus trap cycling Tab/Shift+Tab within panel; add onKeyDown to backdrop for Enter/Space close

title: Admin app layout has no skip-to-main-content link
impact: Keyboard users must Tab through all 8 sidebar navigation links on every page load before reaching main content; violates WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks (Level A)
evidence: RootLayout renders persistent sidebar with 8 navigation links followed by main element; no skip link exists anywhere in the layout
fix_suggestion: Add visually-hidden skip link as first focusable element: Skip to main content; add id=main-content to main element

title: sideEffects field marks all component TypeScript source files as side-effectful blocking tree-shaking
impact: Downstream bundlers cannot eliminate unused components; a consumer importing only hx-button receives all component code; defeats the per-component bundle budget of <5KB
evidence: sideEffects: ["**/*.css", "src/components/**/*.ts"] marks every component .ts file as a side effect
fix_suggestion: Change to sideEffects: ["**/*.css"]; the customElements.define() registration is included in each compiled dist file and is correctly tree-shaken by entry point

title: Package exports, main, module, and types all point to TypeScript source not compiled dist output
impact: External npm consumers receive raw TypeScript with no compiled entry point; package is uninstallable by non-TypeScript bundlers; the dist/ directory listed in files is unreachable via exports
evidence: main, module, types all reference ./src/index.ts; exports map ./ and ./components/* also point to ./src/**/*.ts; dist/ exists but is not mapped
fix_suggestion: Update all four fields to point to dist/: main=./dist/index.js, module=./dist/index.js, types=./dist/index.d.ts; update exports to reference ./dist/**/*.js and ./dist/**/*.d.ts paths

title: Both publishable packages marked private:true permanently blocking npm publish
impact: npm publish and changeset publish both refuse to publish private packages; the entire release pipeline described in CLAUDE.md is dead; no version of the library can ever be published
evidence: packages/hx-library/package.json:4 private:true; packages/hx-tokens/package.json:4 private:true; no publish workflow exists in .github/workflows/
fix_suggestion: Remove private:true from both packages; add publishConfig:{access:public} for scoped public packages; create .github/workflows/release.yml using changesets/action@v1

title: oneEvent() test utility has no timeout allowing tests to hang indefinitely
impact: Any test that awaits an event that never fires blocks the CI pipeline indefinitely with no diagnostic output; a hang is harder to debug than a test failure
evidence: oneEvent() at lines 51-56 returns a Promise with no timeout, no rejection path, and no Promise.race guard; if the event is never dispatched the promise never resolves
fix_suggestion: Add Promise.race with a timeout rejection: Promise.race([eventPromise, new Promise((_, reject) => setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('oneEvent timeout')), timeout))])

title: _shadowQueryAll imported in 3 test files but is not exported from test-utils (dead import, TypeScript blind to test files)
impact: Test files are excluded from tsconfig so the import error is never caught; the name binds to undefined at runtime; any future usage of _shadowQueryAll would throw 'not a function' with no clear error
evidence: hx-card.test.ts:6, hx-radio-group.test.ts:5, hx-alert.test.ts:6 all import _shadowQueryAll; test-utils.ts exports shadowQueryAll (no underscore); tsconfig excludes src/**/*.test.ts
fix_suggestion: Remove the three dead imports; add src/**/*.test.ts to a separate tsconfig.test.json so TypeScript validates test file types

title: Synchronous filesystem write executed on every server render of /health page
impact: saveHealthSnapshot() calls writeFileSync() synchronously on every navigation to /health, blocking the Node.js event loop; in concurrent Next.js rendering can be called multiple times per request
evidence: Line 18: saveHealthSnapshot(components) called directly inside async Server Component render function; comment on line 17 acknowledges: 'In production, this would be triggered by a cron job'
fix_suggestion: Move snapshot writes to a dedicated POST /api/health/snapshot Route Handler triggered by a cron job or explicit UI action; render functions must be pure

title: All admin API routes are completely unauthenticated with no middleware protection
impact: Any unauthenticated client can create issues, modify issue status, and spawn child processes running vitest with full filesystem access
evidence: GET/POST /api/issues, PATCH /api/issues/[id], POST /api/tests/run, GET /api/reviews, GET /api/tokens have zero auth checks; no middleware.ts file exists; no session validation
fix_suggestion: Create apps/admin/src/middleware.ts to validate bearer token or session for all /api/* routes; gate POST /api/tests/run behind deploy-time flag or remove from production build

title: Unsafe dynamic property sort in issues API allows arbitrary field access
impact: Attacker can request ?sort=statusHistory to expose nested object arrays not normally visible; no allowlist restricts sort to intended fields
evidence: Line 34: sort parameter from query string used directly as property key (aRecord[sort]) with no allowlist validation; accepts any field name including internal ones
fix_suggestion: Create allowlist: const ALLOWED_SORTS = ['id','title','severity','status','createdAt','updatedAt']; validate sort against it before use as property key

title: POST and PATCH endpoints accept arbitrary payload sizes with no schema validation
impact: Attacker can POST multi-megabyte strings causing DoS; no length limits on title/description/notes; no sanitization of tags for XSS
evidence: CreateIssueBody and PatchIssueBody interfaces have no length constraints; line 114: (await request.json()) as CreateIssueBody with no Zod or manual validation
fix_suggestion: Add Zod schema validation: const Schema = z.object({title: z.string().min(1).max(255), description: z.string().max(5000), ...}); validate in POST and PATCH handlers before processing

title: No security headers configured on any deployment (missing CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options)
impact: Vulnerable to clickjacking, MIME sniffing, cross-site scripting; missing HSTS means HTTP downgrade attacks possible; healthcare platform compliance gap
evidence: next.config.ts only sets transpilePackages; all three vercel.json files only define X-Robots-Tag; no Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, or Strict-Transport-Security anywhere
fix_suggestion: Add headers() function in next.config.ts with X-Frame-Options:DENY, X-Content-Type-Options:nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, and CSP

title: API error handlers return raw error messages exposing file paths and internal implementation details
impact: ENOENT errors expose filesystem paths; JSON parse errors expose internal data structure; aids attacker reconnaissance
evidence: Catch block lines 84-85 returns error.message directly in detail field; same pattern in all API routes; if ISSUES_PATH is wrong client receives full file path
fix_suggestion: Log full error server-side only; return generic message to client: return NextResponse.json({error:'Internal server error'},{status:500})

title: hx-checkbox native input has tabindex=-1 with no focusable host or label making it unkeyboardable
impact: Checkbox cannot receive keyboard focus through normal Tab navigation; violates WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.1 Keyboard (Level A)
evidence: Native input at line 280 has tabindex=-1; label element at line 261 has no tabindex; no equivalent to hx-radio-group tabIndex management exists for checkboxes; users cannot Tab to and check checkboxes
fix_suggestion: Add tabindex=0 to the label part=control element, or add it to :host with delegatesFocus; remove tabindex=-1 from native input and rely on existing :focus-visible CSS sibling combinator at hx-checkbox.styles.ts:73

title: hx-alert has redundant role=alert and aria-live; static content present on page load is not re-announced
impact: Double announcement on NVDA+Firefox; alerts present at page load are never read by JAWS/NVDA because live region content was there before the region was observed; violates WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.3 Status Messages (AA)
evidence: Line 179 sets both role=alert and aria-live=assertive; role=alert already implies assertive; open property does not conditionally render -- content is in DOM on load even when open=false
fix_suggestion: Remove explicit aria-live attribute; conditionally render alert content only when this.open is true so injecting content triggers screen reader announcement

title: Interactive hx-card aria-label exposes raw URL path instead of meaningful destination description
impact: Screen readers announce 'Navigate to /patient/12345/records' instead of meaningful destination; cryptic URLs are not accessible names; violates WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.4 Link Purpose (A)
evidence: Line 124: aria-label='Navigate to ${this.wcHref}' where wcHref is a raw URL path; this overrides slotted heading content that would be the natural accessible name
fix_suggestion: Remove auto-generated aria-label that exposes raw URL; add documented ariaLabel property that consumers must set when hx-href is used; or render native <a> element when wcHref is set

title: 8 component stylesheets missing prefers-reduced-motion override for CSS transitions
impact: Vestibular-impaired users requesting reduced motion receive animations on every interactive state change; healthcare mandate requires zero WCAG regressions
evidence: hx-button.styles.ts:26-30, hx-card.styles.ts:18-21, hx-text-input.styles.ts, hx-select.styles.ts, hx-textarea.styles.ts, hx-checkbox.styles.ts, hx-radio.styles.ts, hx-switch.styles.ts all have transition: without @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) blocks; only hx-badge implements the guard
fix_suggestion: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .button { transition: none; } } to each component stylesheet; apply same pattern to transform animations in hx-card

title: outline:none baseline in 4 component stylesheets removes focus indicator without :focus-visible guarantee
impact: Browsers or high-contrast modes that do not honor :focus-visible will show zero focus indicator; violates WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.7 Focus Visible (AA)
evidence: hx-switch.styles.ts:45, hx-text-input.styles.ts:104, hx-select.styles.ts:90, hx-textarea.styles.ts:92 all have outline:none on base selector; :focus-visible adds ring but bare outline:none is the baseline
fix_suggestion: Remove bare outline:none; keep only the :focus-visible rule with the outline/box-shadow focus ring; browser handles non-:focus-visible contexts without the override

title: hx-radio-group host has role=radiogroup conflicting with inner shadow DOM fieldset creating duplicate landmark
impact: Screen readers announce the group twice -- once for host role=radiogroup and once for inner fieldset; aria-label on host and legend in shadow DOM both announce the label; JAWS/NVDA handle this inconsistently
evidence: connectedCallback line 128: this.setAttribute('role','radiogroup'); render line 331: also renders <fieldset part=fieldset>; updated() line 148: also sets aria-label on host
fix_suggestion: Choose one pattern: remove role=radiogroup and aria-label from host; rely solely on the inner fieldset+legend in shadow DOM which is the semantically correct HTML approach

title: hx-button--sm minimum height is 32px below the 44px WCAG touch target minimum
impact: Small button variant fails WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 Target Size Minimum (AA); on mobile healthcare devices users may mis-tap adjacent controls
evidence: button--sm lines 53-57 sets min-height: var(--hx-size-8, 2rem) = 32px; WCAG minimum is 44px
fix_suggestion: Raise min-height to 2.75rem (44px) for button--sm; or use transparent padding to achieve 44px interactive area while maintaining visual size

title: TestResultsTable failed test error row uses non-interactive div with onClick handler
impact: Failed test detail rows cannot be keyboard-activated; no role, no tabindex, no onKeyDown; violates WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.1 (A) and SC 4.1.2 (A)
evidence: Line 249: <div onClick={() => hasFailed && toggleError(test.fullName)} cursor-pointer> with no tabindex, no role, no keyboard handler
fix_suggestion: Replace div with <button> element; add aria-expanded={errorOpen} and disabled={!hasFailed} attributes

title: SplitButtonDropdown uses deprecated aria-haspopup=true instead of aria-haspopup=menu
impact: Screen readers may not correctly announce the menu popup; no arrow-key navigation within menu items; focus not returned to trigger on close; violates WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.2 Name Role Value (A)
evidence: Line 70: aria-haspopup=true (deprecated boolean); menu div has role=menu but items have no keyboard navigation (ArrowUp/Down) and no aria-controls linking trigger to menu
fix_suggestion: Change to aria-haspopup=menu; add id to menu div and aria-controls to button; implement ArrowDown/ArrowUp key navigation within menu; return focus to trigger on close

title: No loading.tsx files anywhere in admin app -- all async pages block full render
impact: Dashboard calls 5+ async data sources before sending any HTML; users see blank page until all data is ready; no streaming or progressive loading
evidence: Zero loading.tsx files found across all /app subdirectories; DashboardPage awaits scoreAllComponents(), getAllTestResults(), getManifestStats(), loadIssues(), getTokenStats() sequentially before any render
fix_suggestion: Add loading.tsx at /app/loading.tsx, /app/components/loading.tsx, /app/health/loading.tsx; wrap expensive sections in Suspense boundaries for streaming

title: No error.tsx files anywhere in admin app -- unhandled data fetch errors crash entire pages
impact: If any server data fetch throws (loadIssues, scoreAllComponents, file reads), the entire page crashes with unhandled error; stack traces may be visible to users
evidence: Zero error.tsx files found; roadmap/page.tsx:33 calls loadIssues() with no try/catch; scoreAllComponents() calls in multiple pages are unguarded at page level
fix_suggestion: Add error.tsx files at app root and per major section; each must be a 'use client' Client Component receiving error and reset props per Next.js spec

title: No not-found.tsx for unmatched routes -- Next.js shows generic 404 with no app branding
impact: notFound() called in [tag]/page.tsx falls back to default Next.js 404 page; exposes framework version; poor UX for enterprise healthcare tool
evidence: Zero not-found.tsx files found in admin app; only usage of notFound() at apps/admin/src/app/components/[tag]/page.tsx:43
fix_suggestion: Create apps/admin/src/app/not-found.tsx as a Server Component with branded in-app 404 experience

title: scoreAllComponents runs 17 file-system analyzers per component uncached on every dashboard request
impact: ~200 synchronous file reads per page load on dashboard; same computation re-runs independently on /components and /health pages; cold page loads are measurably slow
evidence: scoreAllComponents() called at page.tsx:36, components/page.tsx:11, health/page.tsx:14 with no shared cache; function runs 17 analyzers × N components including cem-parser, jsdoc-analyzer, bundle-analyzer per call
fix_suggestion: Wrap scoreAllComponents in React.cache() or unstable_cache with 60s revalidate; dynamic import inside the function also re-runs on every call and should be converted to static import

title: Multiple pure display components unnecessarily marked use client preventing server rendering
impact: Client-side JavaScript ships unnecessarily for components with no interactivity; all children of client component boundaries lose server rendering benefits
evidence: ReviewHistory.tsx: 'use client' with only date formatting (no hooks); GlassStatsHero.tsx: 'use client' with only icon+styling (no hooks); RoadmapTimeline.tsx: 'use client' with only list rendering (no hooks)
fix_suggestion: Remove 'use client' from ReviewHistory.tsx, GlassStatsHero.tsx, RoadmapTimeline.tsx; extract only interactive token-nav into a client component in tokens/layout.tsx

title: ComponentCard uses window.location.href bypassing Next.js router and prefetching
impact: Full page reload on navigation; Next.js prefetching does not apply; SPA navigation model broken; the outer <Link> already handles navigation correctly
evidence: Line 77: onClick={() => { window.location.href = `/components/${health.tagName}`; }} inside Details button; card is already wrapped in <Link> at line 33
fix_suggestion: Remove the window.location.href onClick; let the outer Link handle navigation; if a separate click target is needed use useRouter().push() from next/navigation

title: globSync from node:fs is a Node 22+ API but monorepo engines field requires only Node 20
impact: POST /api/tests/run throws TypeError at runtime on Node 20 CI; test runner API silently fails on any deployment using Node 20
evidence: Line 3: import { globSync } from 'node:fs'; node:fs.globSync added in Node v22.0.0; package.json engines field: node>=20.0.0; CI workflow installs Node 20
fix_suggestion: Replace with glob package (already in devDependencies): import { globSync } from 'glob'; or use readdirSync with filter for .test.ts files

title: 6 @query-decorated fields use ! non-null assertion instead of nullable type declarations
impact: @query returns null before firstUpdated; the ! removes undefined from the type, allowing unsafe access before first render; maintenance trap where future code omitting ?. guards compiles but crashes at runtime
evidence: hx-checkbox.ts:119, hx-select.ts:134, hx-radio-group.ts:105, hx-switch.ts:180, hx-text-input.ts:131, hx-textarea.ts:152 all use @query with ! assertion; existing code already uses ?. guards proving fields can be null
fix_suggestion: Remove ! from all six @query declarations; type as HTMLInputElement|null; update _updateValidity calls to use ?? undefined guard

title: 11 render() overrides lack TemplateResult return type annotation
impact: Without explicit return type, uncovered conditional branches returning undefined are not caught by noImplicitReturns; Lit rendering contract is unenforced at compile time
evidence: hx-button.ts:107, hx-alert.ts:172, hx-checkbox.ts:239, hx-select.ts:286, hx-radio-group.ts:320, hx-switch.ts:237, hx-text-input.ts:273, hx-textarea.ts:315, hx-form.ts:280, hx-card.ts:110, hx-radio.ts:104 all omit return type
fix_suggestion: Annotate each as override render(): TemplateResult; import TemplateResult from 'lit'

title: 9 Lit lifecycle overrides use Map<string,unknown> instead of PropertyValues<this>
impact: Missing compile-time validation that changedProperties.has() references real declared property names; typos in property names are silently ignored
evidence: hx-checkbox.ts:132, hx-select.ts:153, hx-radio-group.ts:137,154, hx-radio.ts:68, hx-switch.ts:118, hx-prose.ts:66, hx-text-input.ts:158, hx-textarea.ts:179 all use Map<string,unknown>
fix_suggestion: Import PropertyValues from 'lit'; replace all Map<string,unknown> parameter types with PropertyValues<this>

title: JSON.parse result cast without runtime validation in 9 locations across admin lib files
impact: Malformed JSON or schema changes produce runtime crashes with no type error; no validation of file contents from disk before assuming shape
evidence: loadIssues():61, loadReviewsIndex():83, cem-parser.ts:167, health-history-reader.ts:51, vrt-analyzer.ts:81, test-results-reader.ts:222,279, a11y-analyzer.ts:237 all use as SomeType without validation
fix_suggestion: Use unknown as intermediate type; add type guard function or Zod schema to validate shape before casting; minimum: if (!isExpectedShape(parsed)) throw new Error('Malformed data')

title: getLibraryRoot() function body duplicated identically in 14 separate analyzer files
impact: Library path change requires 14 manual edits; divergence risk is high; represents a maintenance defect violating DRY principle
evidence: function getLibraryRoot(): string { return resolve(process.cwd(), '../../packages/hx-library'); } is identically duplicated in: token-compliance-analyzer.ts, jsdoc-analyzer.ts, code-quality-analyzer.ts, health-scorer.ts, maintainability-analyzer.ts, story-coverage-analyzer.ts, dx-analyzer.ts, type-safety-analyzer.ts, lit-best-practices-analyzer.ts, security-analyzer.ts, drupal-readiness-analyzer.ts, bundle-analyzer.ts, source-analyzer.ts, a11y-analyzer.ts
fix_suggestion: Create apps/admin/src/lib/paths.ts exporting getLibraryRoot(); import it in all 14 files; also extract duplicated readSource() function present in 6 of the same files

title: Astro docs Hero component reads NEXT_PUBLIC_ env var prefix that Astro never exposes
impact: Storybook link on production docs homepage always renders as localhost:3151; NEXT_PUBLIC_ is Next.js-specific; Astro requires PUBLIC_ prefix
evidence: Line 19: import.meta.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_HELIX_STORYBOOK_URL ?? 'http://localhost:3151'; Astro only exposes PUBLIC_* to client; apps/docs/.env.local also defines NEXT_PUBLIC_HELIX_STORYBOOK_URL not PUBLIC_HELIX_STORYBOOK_URL
fix_suggestion: Change env var reference to import.meta.env.PUBLIC_HELIX_STORYBOOK_URL; rename variable in .env.local and Vercel env dashboard accordingly

title: No npm publish workflow exists in CI -- release pipeline is entirely manual
impact: Publishing requires manual local execution; no @next channel from main; no automated version PR via changesets; CLAUDE.md release pipeline is dead letter
evidence: Only ci.yml and ci-matrix.yml exist in .github/workflows/; no release.yml, publish.yml, or changeset.yml; changeset config exists but no automation
fix_suggestion: Create .github/workflows/release.yml using changesets/action@v1; configure to create version PR on push to main and publish to npm on merge; add NODE_AUTH_TOKEN from GitHub Secrets

title: Missing preserveModules:true in vite.config.ts causes cross-component shared chunks
impact: Consumer importing only hx-radio-group also receives hx-radio implementation (13.8KB shared chunk); per-component import isolation is impossible without this setting
evidence: dist/shared/hx-radio-CspCxsJ2.js bundles both hx-radio and hx-radio-group; chunkFileNames config at line 42 confirms shared chunks are expected; no preserveModules:true in output config
fix_suggestion: Add preserveModules:true and preserveModulesRoot:'src' to rollupOptions.output; this creates one file per source module enabling true per-module tree-shaking

title: Slot-tracking booleans use plain class fields with unconditional requestUpdate() instead of @state
impact: requestUpdate() called unconditionally on every slotchange even when state did not change; fragile pattern breaks silently if manual call is ever removed; same issue in hx-textarea.ts and hx-card.ts
evidence: Lines 135-136: _hasLabelSlot and _hasErrorSlot are plain private fields; _handleLabelSlotChange at line 147 calls this.requestUpdate() unconditionally; hx-card.ts:65 uses mutable plain object with same pattern
fix_suggestion: Decorate with @state(); remove explicit requestUpdate() calls; Lit's dirty-checking then only re-renders when value actually changes

title: vitest.config.ts has no coverage thresholds enforcing the 80% mandate
impact: npm run test exits 0 even if coverage drops to 0%; the CLAUDE.md mandate of 80%+ coverage is documented but not enforced; any developer can remove tests and CI still passes
evidence: coverage config has provider, enabled, include, exclude, reporter, reportsDirectory -- but no thresholds key; current 94.85% is healthy but unenforced
fix_suggestion: Add thresholds: { lines: 80, functions: 80, branches: 80, statements: 80 } to the coverage config in vitest.config.ts

title: globals.css shimmer animations run infinitely with no prefers-reduced-motion override
impact: Vestibular-impaired users requesting reduced motion receive continuous infinite shimmer animations throughout test runs; inline style animation in TestProgressBar also bypasses CSS media query override
evidence: bar-pass, bar-warn, bar-fail, bar-coverage all use animation: bar-shimmer 3s ease-in-out infinite; animate-resolved-shimmer uses resolved-shimmer 3s infinite; TestProgressBar.tsx:61 applies animation via inline style which has higher specificity than media query
fix_suggestion: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .bar-pass,.bar-warn,.bar-fail,.bar-coverage,.animate-resolved-shimmer { animation: none; } } after line 146 in globals.css; move TestProgressBar animation to CSS class

title: TestRunnerPanel catch blocks silently swallow streaming errors with no user feedback
impact: Network failures, server crashes, and CORS errors return UI to idle state with no explanation; user cannot distinguish test completion from silent failure; violates project 'No silent failures' mandate
evidence: Line 168: catch {} wrapping the entire streaming fetch; line 159: catch { // Cached results may not be ready yet }; line 163: catch { // Skip malformed events }; none set error state or show user message
fix_suggestion: Add error state variable; set user-readable message in outer catch; render error message below progress bar; keep inner catch for malformed SSE events only

title: IssueDetailPanel status update PATCH failure is silently swallowed
impact: When PATCH to /api/issues/{id} fails, UI reverts visually but user receives zero feedback; no toast, no inline error, no retry affordance; violates 'No silent failures' mandate
evidence: Lines 107-109: catch { // Silently fail - user can retry }; user has no indication that the status update failed
fix_suggestion: Add error state to panel; display inline error message below status select when PATCH fails: 'Update failed. Please try again.'

title: aria-describedby in hx-text-input includes helpTextId when help element is not rendered during error state
impact: aria-describedby references non-existent DOM element; assistive technology treats broken reference as if no description exists; help text silently lost when error is shown
evidence: Lines 283-286: describedBy includes this._helpTextId when this.helpText is set regardless of hasError; but help text div at line 352 only renders when !hasError; same pattern in hx-textarea.ts:325 and hx-select.ts:301
fix_suggestion: Change to: [hasError ? this._errorId : null, this.helpText && !hasError ? this._helpTextId : null].filter(Boolean).join(' ') || undefined

title: SVG resolution progress circle in dashboard has no accessible label
impact: Screen reader users cannot determine resolution rate from the SVG ring; violates WCAG 2.1 SC 1.1.1 Non-text Content (A)
evidence: Lines 416-444: <svg viewBox='0 0 36 36'> with no aria-label, no <title>, no role=img; percentage text is in an absolutely positioned span outside SVG
fix_suggestion: Add aria-hidden=true to SVG; ensure sibling span is readable in context; add sr-only span: Resolution rate: {resolutionPct}%

title: Health score progress bars convey status by color alone with no accessible labeling
impact: Color-only status indication violates WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.1 Use of Color (A); users who cannot perceive color difference cannot determine component health from bars
evidence: Lines 510-519: bars change color based on score (emerald/amber/red) with no role, no aria-label, no aria-valuenow; health bars have no accessible representation
fix_suggestion: Add role=progressbar, aria-valuenow={h.overallScore}, aria-valuemin=0, aria-valuemax=100, aria-label='{h.tagName} health score: {h.overallScore}%' to each bar container

title: Recharts RadarChart SVG has no accessible text alternative
impact: Screen reader users cannot access component health radar data; chart is purely visual with no table or description fallback; violates WCAG 2.1 SC 1.1.1 (A)
evidence: ResponsiveContainer wraps RadarChart with no aria-label, no figure/figcaption, no sr-only data table; Recharts does not auto-generate accessible SVG structure
fix_suggestion: Wrap in <figure aria-label='Component health radar'> with <figcaption className='sr-only'> containing a data table of dimensions, scores, and confidence values

title: hx-switch aria-labelledby can reference an empty element when no label property or slot is provided
impact: Switch with no label has no accessible name; violates WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.2 Name Role Value (A)
evidence: Lines 262-292: aria-labelledby always set to this._labelId; when hasLabel is false, an empty is rendered with no content; empty aria-labelledby is equivalent to no label
fix_suggestion: Only set aria-labelledby when label or slotted content exists; add console warning when neither is present; consider adding aria-label fallback property

title: Admin sidebar nav element has no aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation landmarks
impact: Screen reader users navigating by landmarks encounter an unlabeled navigation region; multiple navigation regions require unique labels per ARIA best practice
evidence: <nav> at line 63 has no aria-label or aria-labelledby; when pages have multiple navigation regions this causes ambiguity in screen reader landmark navigation
fix_suggestion: Add aria-label='Main navigation' to the nav element at line 63

title: External target=_blank links give no warning of new tab behavior
impact: Unexpected context changes (new windows) must be communicated in advance; screen reader users may be disoriented when focus shifts to new window
evidence: Sidebar links at lines 100 and 141 use target=_blank rel=noopener noreferrer with no aria-label suffix or sr-only text indicating new tab; same pattern in page.tsx:155,198
fix_suggestion: Add aria-label='Open Storybook (opens in new tab)' or (opens in new tab) to each external link

title: Docs site skip link targets #_top (page top) instead of main content area
impact: Skip link sends keyboard users to page top not past navigation; bypass blocks function is not achieved; violates WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.1 (A)
evidence: Line 9: PAGE_TITLE_ID = '_top'; line 16 renders <a href='#_top'>; this skips to page top not to main article content
fix_suggestion: Change PAGE_TITLE_ID to reference Starlight main content ID (e.g., #starlight__content or add id=main-content to main element)

title: Public docs site marked noindex in astro.config.mjs preventing search engine indexing
impact: Public documentation site is invisible to search engines; developers cannot find the library documentation via web search; contradicts purpose of public docs
evidence: head: [{tag:'meta',attrs:{name:'robots',content:'noindex, nofollow'}}] in Starlight config at lines 57-58; docs site is the public-facing entry point for the library
fix_suggestion: Remove the noindex meta tag from astro.config.mjs head array; docs site should be indexable and followable

title: robots.txt blocks all crawlers on both admin and docs sites
impact: Admin blocking is intentional but docs blocking prevents any SEO; docs site content cannot be discovered via web search
evidence: Both apps/admin/public/robots.txt and apps/docs/public/robots.txt: User-agent: * / Disallow: /
fix_suggestion: Keep admin robots.txt as-is (appropriate for internal tool); change docs robots.txt to Allow: / to enable crawling of public documentation

title: Multiple empty alt attributes on technology logos in docs site serving as content identifiers
impact: Technology logos with alt='' are treated as decorative; screen reader users miss technology stack information; 25+ instances across stack showcase components
evidence: StackBundleSize.astro:179, StackIntegrationMap.astro:175,190,205,235,250, StackVisualization.astro:208-265, StackComparisons.astro:207,286,478, tech-stack.astro:568-572 all use alt=''
fix_suggestion: Add descriptive alt text to technology logos: alt='Lit 3.x logo', alt='Vitest testing framework logo', alt='Storybook component playground logo', etc.

title: No per-page metadata on any admin route except root layout
impact: Every admin sub-page shares the generic root layout title; browser tabs, history, and bookmarks show no page-specific information; dynamic component pages have no component-specific title
evidence: health/page.tsx, tests/page.tsx, components/page.tsx, pipeline/page.tsx, tokens/page.tsx, roadmap/page.tsx, hooks/page.tsx all have no metadata export; only layout.tsx has Metadata
fix_suggestion: Add export const metadata: Metadata = { title: 'Page Name — HELIX Admin' } to each page; use generateMetadata for dynamic [tag] pages

title: Dashboard page.tsx is 992 lines with inline helper components and mixed async data loading
impact: File is difficult to test, code-split, or review; data loading, presentation logic, and helper components all co-located; KpiCard and SectionCard defined inline
evidence: 992 lines; KpiCard at line 903, SectionCard at line 943 defined inline; 5 async data calls before any rendering; Storybook and Docs CTA blocks each 40+ lines
fix_suggestion: Extract KpiCard, SectionCard into /src/components/dashboard/; extract CTA blocks and Platform Health Pulse into separate Server Components; aim for <300 lines per file

title: Hardcoded brand hex colors in admin dashboard violate design token requirement
impact: Six hardcoded hex values (#FF4785, #AB37AC, #8B5CF6, #F97316) create multiple sources of truth; values repeated verbatim in layout.tsx; violates zero-tolerance 'no hardcoded values' policy
evidence: Lines 160,168,175,179,187,194: inline style props with raw hex strings; comment @design-system-approved: ADMIN-001 is not a substitute for token usage; also in layout.tsx:120,126,137,161,175
fix_suggestion: Extract brand colors to Tailwind CSS variables or a shared constants file; apply via className; remove @design-system-approved comment workaround

title: hx-radio-group hasError computation ignores _hasErrorSlot state
impact: When consumer uses error slot without setting error attribute, fieldset--error class and aria-invalid are not applied; slot-only error pattern is silently broken
evidence: Line 321: const hasError = !!this.error; but _hasErrorSlot is tracked at line 107 with @state() and never consulted in render(); inconsistent with hx-text-input which uses !!this.error || this._hasErrorSlot
fix_suggestion: Change line 321 to: const hasError = !!this.error || this._hasErrorSlot; add aria-invalid and aria-describedby to fieldset when hasError is true

title: TrendChart SVG linearGradient uses static id=gradient causing global ID collision when multiple charts render
impact: Multiple TrendChart instances on same page share the same gradient definition; whichever rendered last wins; charts display wrong gradient colors
evidence: Line 89: <linearGradient id='gradient'> is static string; SVG url(#gradient) IDs are global in document scope; component page may render multiple TrendChart instances per-component
fix_suggestion: Use React 18 useId(): const id = useId(); const gradientId = `trend-gradient-${id}`; reference gradientId in both linearGradient and stroke url

title: 100ms setInterval triggers 10 React re-renders per second during test runs
impact: Unnecessary CPU overhead for a timer that updates display to only 1 decimal second precision; 60% more re-renders than needed
evidence: Lines 87-89: setInterval(() => setElapsed(...), 100); elapsed displayed as (elapsed/1000).toFixed(1)s -- one decimal second resolution; 100ms interval is invisible at this precision
fix_suggestion: Increase interval to 250ms (4 updates/sec vs 10); display has only 1 decimal precision so 250ms updates are fully sufficient

title: O(n²) array allocation for streaming test results via spread accumulation
impact: With 550+ tests: ~151,000 array element allocations across all updates; the results array is only used for results.length which is already tracked by passed+failed counters
evidence: Line 131: setResults((prev) => [...prev, result]) on every test result event; after 550 tests: 550+549+...+1 elements allocated; results state only used as results.length at line 218
fix_suggestion: Remove results state entirely; use passed+failed counter for progress bar denominator: const completedCount = passed + failed

title: getTestResultsForComponent called N times in render loop re-reading same file on each call
impact: 12 components × 1 file read each = 12 unnecessary disk reads of the same test-results.json file per page load
evidence: Line 43: healthScores.map calls getTestResultsForComponent(health.tagName) inside JSX; test-results.json contains all components in a single file that is opened and parsed 12 times
fix_suggestion: Call getAllTestResults() once before the map; pass component data from pre-loaded results: allResults?.components.find(c => c.component === health.tagName)

title: Changeset access set to restricted blocking public npm publish
impact: Combined with private:true on packages, publishing is blocked at two layers; restricted access requires paid npm org membership to install
evidence: config.json line 8: access:restricted; intended as public enterprise component library per CLAUDE.md
fix_suggestion: Change to access:public; add ignore list for private app packages (docs, storybook, admin, mcp-*) that should never be published

title: CI VRT step uses sleep 5 instead of readiness check creating fragile race condition
impact: On slow or loaded GitHub Actions runners Storybook server may not be ready; VRT tests fail with connection refused producing false-negative CI failures
evidence: Lines 84-88: npx http-server ... & sleep 5; npm run test:vrt -- 5 second sleep is not a reliable readiness check
fix_suggestion: Replace sleep 5 with: npx wait-on http://localhost:3151 --timeout 30000 before running VRT tests

title: CI matrix tests Node 18 which violates the engines field declaring Node >=20 required
impact: Testing on an unsupported runtime creates false confidence; if Node 18 support is not intended, bugs found only on 18 are irrelevant noise
evidence: node-version: [18, 20, 22] at line 33; package.json engines: {node: '>=20.0.0'}; Node 18 reached end-of-life April 2025
fix_suggestion: Remove 18 from matrix if Node 18 is not supported; or update engines field to >=18.0.0 if Node 18 support is intentional; pick one and be consistent

title: 21 instances of setTimeout(r, 50) used as async synchronization creating flaky test risk
impact: On loaded CI machines 50ms may be insufficient for Chromium to complete focus events or slot changes; also adds ~1 second of artificial delay to test suite
evidence: 21 occurrences across hx-button.test.ts:175,249,271, hx-card.test.ts:170,204, hx-select.test.ts:234,252,270,294,450, hx-radio-group.test.ts:309,608, and others
fix_suggestion: Replace setTimeout(r, 50) with await el.updateComplete for Lit property changes; use oneEvent(slot, 'slotchange') for slot events; use page.waitForFunction for focus state

title: Admin app has zero test coverage -- health scorer, grading algorithm, and API routes completely untested
impact: calculateGrade() multi-branch penalty logic, mergeResults() test aggregation, parseVitestJson() schema parsing -- all complex business logic is completely untested; a bug silently mis-grades all components
evidence: No vitest.config.ts, no jest.config.*, no *.test.* files, no test scripts in apps/admin/package.json; health-scorer.ts:569 lines, test-results-reader.ts:326 lines, api/tests/run/route.ts:299 lines all untested
fix_suggestion: Add vitest.config.ts to admin app; write unit tests for calculateGrade(), parseVitestJson(), getCoverageForComponent(), and mergeResults() at minimum

title: hx-button keyboard tests call btn.click() manually defeating the keyboard behavior being tested
impact: Tests pass even if keyboard handler is broken; btn.click() is what fires hx-click, not the keyboard event; keyboard activation is not actually verified
evidence: Line 183: btn.dispatchEvent(new KeyboardEvent('keydown', {key:'Enter'})); btn.click(); -- the click() call is what resolves oneEvent, keyboard dispatch is a no-op
fix_suggestion: Remove btn.click(); dispatch keyboard event only and await el.updateComplete; test should verify that keyboard event alone triggers hx-click without manual click

title: hx-radio internal ID uses stale wc-radio- prefix instead of hx-radio- (missed in token migration)
impact: Generated DOM IDs like wc-radio-abc1234 are inconsistent with hx- convention; confusing in DevTools and AT debugging; inconsistent with all other components
evidence: Line 79: private _inputId = `wc-radio-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 9)}`; all other components use hx- prefix
fix_suggestion: Change to hx-radio-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 9)}

title: hx-radio-group JSDoc references <wc-radio> and <wc-radio-group> tag names
impact: @slot documentation on line 15 uses wc-radio generating incorrect CEM slot docs; contributes to consumer confusion
evidence: Line 9: 'manages a set of <wc-radio> children'; line 15: @slot - <wc-radio> elements; hx-radio.ts:8 also references <wc-radio-group>
fix_suggestion: Replace <wc-radio> with <hx-radio> and <wc-radio-group> with <hx-radio-group> in all three locations

title: @keyframes wc-badge-pulse uses old wc- prefix in hx-badge component stylesheet
impact: Keyframe name is inconsistent with hx- naming convention; maintenance hazard when searching for animations
evidence: Line 92: @keyframes wc-badge-pulse; line 103 references it as wc-badge-pulse in animation property
fix_suggestion: Rename to hx-badge-pulse in both @keyframes declaration and animation property reference

title: Magic number 550 used as estimated test count with no explanation or constant name
impact: Estimate silently drifts as test suite grows (actual count is now 563); progress bar shows inaccurate percentages on first run
evidence: Line 188: const totalEstimate = initialResults?.totalTests ?? 550; no constant name, no comment explaining the value's source
fix_suggestion: Extract to ESTIMATED_TEST_COUNT = 550 constant with comment explaining it mirrors the test suite size; ideally derive from the 'started' SSE event's totalFiles count

title: as unknown as Node double-cast in test-utils for axe-core bypasses type safety
impact: Masks genuine type incompatibility between ShadowRoot and axe-core Node parameter type; future axe-core upgrades may break silently
evidence: Line 77: axe.default.run(context as unknown as Node, {}); context is ShadowRoot|HTMLElement; axe types do not include ShadowRoot despite runtime support
fix_suggestion: Replace with @ts-expect-error with explanatory comment and issue link; @ts-expect-error is safer than as unknown as because it fails compilation if the underlying incompatibility is ever fixed

title: Husky prepare script uses legacy require() syntax that fails silently in ESM context
impact: npm ci runs prepare script which always throws (ESM/CJS mismatch) and swallows the error; git hooks may not be properly installed for new developers
evidence: Line 72: node -e "try { require('husky').install() } catch (e) {}"; project is type:module; husky v9 has no .install() method and is not CJS-importable
fix_suggestion: Replace with: prepare: husky -- this is the correct husky v9 setup command per official docs

title: GitHub Actions pinned to mutable major version tags not immutable SHAs
impact: Action maintainers can push breaking changes to @v4 tag; supply chain integrity risk for a healthcare project
evidence: uses: actions/checkout@v4, actions/setup-node@v4, actions/upload-artifact@v4 throughout ci.yml and ci-matrix.yml
fix_suggestion: Pin all actions to full commit SHAs; add .github/dependabot.yml with package-ecosystem: github-actions for automated updates

title: Docs and Storybook apps have no lint script -- turbo lint silently skips them
impact: TypeScript and Astro code in docs and Storybook apps is never linted in CI; Turborepo silently skips packages without the requested task
evidence: apps/docs/package.json has no lint script; apps/storybook/package.json has no lint script; turbo lint skips both silently
fix_suggestion: Add lint: eslint src/ to both apps and ensure eslint.config.* exists for each

title: storybook:build task in turbo.json is dead code -- no package defines this script
impact: Dead configuration creates confusion; task is never executed; root build:storybook script uses 'build' task not 'storybook:build'
evidence: turbo.json lines 34-37 define storybook:build task; no package.json in monorepo has a storybook:build script; npm run build:storybook runs turbo build --filter=@helix/storybook
fix_suggestion: Remove the storybook:build task definition from turbo.json lines 34-37

title: Docs site title inconsistency between config (HELiX) and expected branding (HELIX)
impact: Minor branding inconsistency affects page titles in browser tabs and search results
evidence: astro.config.mjs line 42: title: 'HELiX' (lowercase i); other references use HELIX consistently
fix_suggestion: Standardize to 'HELIX Documentation' throughout astro.config.mjs

title: CLAUDE.md test count is wildly outdated reporting 112 tests when actual count is 563
impact: New team members and CI scoring systems receive incorrect baseline expectations; documentation also references old wc- tag names
evidence: CLAUDE.md states '112 tests across wc-button (30), wc-card (35), wc-text-input (47)'; test-results.json shows numTotalTests:563 across 14 components with hx- prefix
fix_suggestion: Update CLAUDE.md to reflect: 563 tests, 14 components, all using hx- prefix naming

title: hx-prose lg size variant has no test coverage
impact: The lg variant CSS custom property mapping is unverified; a regression in size variant logic would not be caught
evidence: hx-prose.ts:86-89 defines sizeMap with sm/base/lg values; hx-prose.test.ts:40 tests sm reflection; lg variant has zero test coverage
fix_suggestion: Add test: size='lg' reflects attribute correctly and applies --hx-prose-font-size custom property with lg value
